message:
fix(runtime): replace tokio::select! with explicit polling for reliable shutdown

The previous select!-based patterns in the runtime capture loop, metrics
listener, and IPC server could hang during shutdown when branches held
open futures that never resolved (e.g., idle client connections).

Changes:
- runtime.rs: restructure capture task from select!-based to explicit
  time-driven tick loop with shutdown check, adding configurable
  capture_timeout to tailer config for deterministic shutdown
- metrics.rs: replace select! on listener.accept() with timeout() +
  explicit shutdown_rx check, ensuring metrics server exits cleanly
  even when no new connections arrive
- ipc.rs: add connection_tasks.abort_all() during shutdown to prevent
  hangs from idle client connections, plus regression test verifying
  the shutdown-hang fix

These changes prevent a class of shutdown deadlocks observed when the
mux server is under load with many idle monitoring connections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
